sources.label: StockTitan AMKR news feed (latest items May 21 Investor Day, May 19 Peoria AZ 67-acre expansion; nothing Jun 4-9)
Investing.com - Amkor stock hit all-time high $79.49 (stock now ~13% below that level)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new fundamental news since Jun 6 and today's +1.1% is drift; the ~13% consolidation off the $79.49 all-time high with the HBM/chiplet packaging thesis intact modestly restores asymmetry, but not enough to re-rank a name already at 6 after a 60.9% YTD run.
verdicts.bull: Only US-scale OSAT with a high-volume advanced packaging campus coming in Peoria AZ; record Q1 (+27% y/y) and strong Q2 guide show AI packaging attach is real and accelerating.
verdicts.neutral: Stock is digesting a 60.9% YTD rerating; the pullback from $79.49 to $69 resets some froth without changing the bottleneck case either way.
verdicts.bear: OSAT pricing power is structurally thinner than front-end; if CoWoS-class capacity at TSMC/foundries catches up, Amkor's scarcity premium compresses quickly.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results in late July confirming the $1.75-1.85B sales guide and AI/HPC packaging mix
verdicts.primaryRisk: OSAT cyclicality and margin pressure if AI packaging demand pauses after the YTD rerating

verdicts.rankRationale: On this crowding-sensitive alpha list the 60.9% YTD move means the 'less crowded' edge is fading, but the week's ~13% drawdown from the all-time high with thesis intact partially rebuilds the asymmetry, netting to a hold at rank 11 with no fresh news since Jun 6.
verdicts.bull: Packaging/test leverage to AI chiplets and co-packaged optics modules remains under-modeled versus front-end equipment names; Q1 record results validate the ramp.
verdicts.neutral: UBS sits at Neutral with an $80 target near the prior high; valuation discovery has largely happened, so the trade is now execution, not discovery.
verdicts.bear: The name is no longer hidden after a 60%+ YTD run and an Investor Day spotlight; further upside requires beat-and-raise quarters, and OSAT pricing is the weak link.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Design-win or capacity disclosures around co-packaged optics and 2.5D/3D packaging following the May Investor Day
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding-driven de-rating: post-ATH momentum sellers plus any AI capex digestion hits a now well-owned OSAT first

verdicts.rankRationale: Packaging-scarcity bucket positioning is unchanged: no news since Jun 6, +1.1% day move is noise, and yesterday's hold already balanced bottleneck exposure against the YTD rerating.
verdicts.bull: Advanced substrate and 2.5D packaging scarcity persists into 2027; Peoria AZ build gives Amkor the only US high-volume advanced-packaging OSAT footprint, a strategic moat under reshoring policy.
verdicts.neutral: Rank 9 already reflects measured credit for scarcity exposure net of the 60.9% YTD move and $17B cap; nothing this week argues for a change.
verdicts.bear: Scarcity rents in OSAT historically mean-revert fast as capacity adds land; the $1B convert funds growth but signals heavy capex ahead of uncertain pricing.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Peoria, Arizona campus milestones and any anchor-customer announcement for US advanced packaging
verdicts.primaryRisk: Packaging capacity catch-up across OSATs/foundries eroding the scarcity premium embedded in the score
